decisions.title: Why Directory-Level Isolation
decisions.description: Each agent task gets its own working directory (a git worktree). This provides filesystem-level isolation: one agent's file modifications cannot interfere with another's. It also enables parallel work on different branches of the same repository without the overhead of full clones. The isolation boundary is the directory, which the operating system already enforces.
decisions.alternatives: Container-based isolation (Docker) provides stronger guarantees including process and network isolation, but adds significant overhead and complexity. Virtual environments isolate dependencies but not file changes. Git worktrees provide exactly the right level of isolation for code-editing agents: separate file states with shared git history.

zh.title: 为什么采用目录级隔离
zh.description: 每个 Agent 任务获得自己的工作目录（一个 git worktree）。这提供了文件系统级别的隔离：一个 Agent 的文件修改不会干扰另一个。它还支持在同一仓库的不同分支上并行工作，而不需要完整克隆的开销。隔离边界就是目录，这是操作系统本身就在执行的。

decisions.title: Why Separate Task Goals from Worktree Directories
decisions.description: The task definition (what to do) is stored separately from the worktree (where to do it). A task might say 'fix bug #42' while the worktree is at '/tmp/worktrees/fix-bug-42'. This separation allows the same task to be retried in a fresh worktree if the first attempt fails, and allows worktrees to be cleaned up without losing the task history.
decisions.alternatives: Coupling the task to a specific directory is simpler but creates problems: if the directory is corrupted, the task metadata is lost. Storing everything in the worktree also makes cleanup dangerous — deleting the worktree deletes the task record. Separation of concerns applies here just as it does in software design.

zh.title: 为什么将任务目标与工作目录分离
zh.description: 任务定义（做什么）与工作目录（在哪里做）分开存储。一个任务可能是「修复 bug #42」，而工作目录在 '/tmp/worktrees/fix-bug-42'。这种分离使得同一任务可以在新的工作目录中重试（如果首次尝试失败），也允许清理工作目录而不丢失任务历史。

decisions.title: Why Explicit Worktree Lifecycle Management
decisions.description: Worktrees are created, used, and destroyed through explicit tool calls (EnterWorktree, ExitWorktree). This makes the lifecycle visible in the agent's conversation history and auditable. The agent must consciously enter and exit a worktree, which prevents accidental modifications to the wrong directory and makes the agent's actions reproducible.
decisions.alternatives: Implicit worktree management (automatically creating worktrees for each task) reduces boilerplate but hides important state transitions. The agent might not realize which worktree it is in, leading to cross-contamination. Explicit lifecycle management trades convenience for clarity and safety.

zh.title: 为什么需要显式的工作目录生命周期管理
zh.description: 工作目录通过显式的工具调用（EnterWorktree、ExitWorktree）来创建、使用和销毁。这使得生命周期在 Agent 的对话历史中可见且可审计。Agent 必须有意识地进入和退出工作目录，防止对错误目录的意外修改，并使 Agent 的操作可重现。
